title: IIoT-enabled digital twin for legacy and smart factory machines with LLM integration

agents.description: The Supervisor Agent acts as the central coordinator ('brain') of the framework, processing user queries and assigning tasks to the appropriate specialized sub-agents based on predefined categories such as visualization, fault diagnosis, or general machine expertise. It is fine-tuned on a synthetic dataset to classify diverse user inputs.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: A synthetic dataset generated by feeding relevant documentation into ChatGPT and guiding the model with few examples to create realistic labeled inputs. Labels included categories such as 'Visualization', 'Ultimaker', 'Stratasys', 'Mockpanel', and 'Error'. 80% used for training, 20% for validation.

agents.description: The Visualization Agent dynamically generates static charts and graphs to visualize data from both legacy and smart machines based on streaming real-time IIoT data via the MTConnect protocol. It uses Microsoft's LIDA libraries for rendering and an additional GPT-4o-based summarization layer to analyze visual trends and extract meaningful insights.

The Machine Expertise Agent manages complex user queries by dynamically retrieving and synthesizing text and image-based information from a structured knowledge base using a RAG approach. It performs cosine similarity search to retrieve relevant text and image metadata from a vector database and generates expert-level guidance tailored to user inputs.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: Fine-tuned with domain-specific knowledge including proprietary machine manuals, maintenance logs, and calibration records not publicly available (e.g., in-house Mockpanel manual). RAG is used to integrate specialized knowledge for context-aware answers.

agents.description: The Fault Diagnosis and Control Agent performs real-time fault diagnosis for both legacy and smart machines using IIoT-enabled digital twin data. It continuously observes real-time sensor data, queries a structured knowledge base with past fault logs and diagnostic decision trees, and integrates user inputs to diagnose machine faults and recommend corrective actions. It also enables remote machine control by calling predefined functions from a command library based on natural language commands.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: Fine-tuned using annotated datasets that include common fault scenarios, error codes, and machine-specific diagnostic data. A command library with predefined machine control functions was used to train the control agent to map natural language commands to function calls.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: A Supervisor Agent acts as the central coordinator, receiving user queries and classifying them into predefined categories (visualization, fault diagnosis, machine expertise). Based on classification, it delegates tasks to one of three specialized sub-agents: Visualization Agent, Machine Expertise Agent, or Fault Diagnosis and Control Agent. Each agent operates within its intended scope and returns results to the user through the digital twin interface.

multi_agent_coordination_strategy.multi_agent_coordination_strategy_descri: The Supervisor Agent centrally coordinates task assignment to specialized sub-agents. Each sub-agent handles a specific domain (visualization, expertise retrieval, fault diagnosis/control) and operates cooperatively to fulfill user requests. The supervisor classifies queries and routes them to the appropriate agent, which then processes and returns results.

human_integration.human_integration_description: Operators interact with the digital twin system through voice commands or text inputs via an AI-driven MetaHuman avatar. The avatar uses Whisper AI for speech recognition and OpenAI TTS for verbal responses. Users can query machine status, request visualizations, report faults, and issue machine control commands in natural language. The Fault Diagnosis Agent allows users to refine diagnoses by supplying additional contextual information or validating recommendations iteratively.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Real-time monitoring, fault diagnosis, and machine control for legacy and smart manufacturing machines in a smart factory environment, integrating IIoT data streams with LLM-powered digital twins.
retrieval.problem_description: Digital twins can process real-time data but often overlook unstructured data (machine manuals, fault logs). LLMs trained on static datasets cannot adapt to real-time factory floor operations. Integrating legacy machines (lacking connectivity) with modern smart machines into a unified IIoT ecosystem is challenging. Operators must manually consult documentation to troubleshoot faults, causing delays. There is a gap in integrating LLMs with IIoT and digital twins for real-time anomaly detection, fault analysis, and monitoring.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A multi-agent LLM framework integrated within a digital twin of a smart manufacturing lab. Real-time data from legacy machines (via computer vision and OCR), smart machines (via RESTful APIs and MTConnect), and building sensors is standardized using the MTConnect protocol. Four specialized agents (Supervisor, Visualization, Machine Expertise, Fault Diagnosis/Control) collaborate to process user queries, visualize data, retrieve domain knowledge via RAG, and diagnose/correct faults. An AI-driven MetaHuman avatar enables voice and text interaction with the digital twin.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: A case study was conducted in the Smart Manufacturing Lab at the University of Cincinnati, demonstrating the framework with legacy machine artifacts (Mockpanel), smart machines (Ultimaker S5, Stratasys F170 3D printers), and building sensors. Controlled experiments evaluated Supervisor Agent classification performance, Machine Expertise Agent RAG accuracy across multiple LLMs, and Fault Diagnosis Agent effectiveness in resolving 3D printing issues.

decision_quality_improvement: Fine-tuned GPT-4o-mini Supervisor Agent achieved accuracy of 82% and F1 score of 92% in query classification, substantially outperforming base model versions.

Machine Expertise Agent with GPT-4o-mini achieved accuracy of 0.903 ± 0.036 and LLM-Eval scores of 0.922 ± 0.047 (GPT-4o) and 0.925 ± 0.020 (Gemini), best among tested models for subjective evaluation.
Llama 3.1 (405B) achieved highest accuracy of 0.916 ± 0.038 in objective questions for Machine Expertise Agent.
Fault Diagnosis Agent successfully identified suboptimal bed temperature (45°C vs optimal 60°C for PLA) and recommended corrective action, resulting in significant print quality improvement.
RAG-based retrieval enhanced contextual accuracy and reduced hallucinations compared to conventional static LLMs.
response_time_impact: Legacy machine and building sensor data acquisition and processing cycle maintained an average latency of approximately 1.3 seconds, comparable to near-real-time operational monitoring.
Digital twin system enables operators to toggle between machine datasets with a latency of 1-2 seconds.
reliability_metrics: Supervisor Agent fine-tuned GPT-4o-mini: precision and accuracy near-optimal, accuracy 82%, F1 score 92%.
Machine Expertise Agent experiments repeated five times with same question pool to assess robustness; GPT-4o-mini accuracy 0.903 ± 0.036.
Total API cost for 30 queries approximately $0.12 for LLM and $0.22 for audio processing.

ce_technique_effectiveness.retrieval-augmented-generation: RAG improved contextual accuracy and reduced hallucinations; GPT-4o-mini with RAG achieved accuracy 0.903 and LLM-Eval 0.922, outperforming larger models like Mixtral 8x22B (accuracy 0.806) on subjective tasks.
ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Prompt engineering with detailed instructions and contextual information was necessary to complement fine-tuning, as fine-tuning alone was insufficient for complex manufacturing queries due to token processing limitations.
ce_technique_effectiveness.iterative-reasoning: Fault Diagnosis Agent iteratively narrows down fault causes through closed-loop interaction with user feedback and real-time data, enabling root cause identification and corrective action execution.

failure_modes: Fine-tuning alone was not always sufficient to ensure optimal model performance due to complexity of manufacturing queries and inherent limitations of token processing.
Defects persisting in ongoing 3D prints due to inherent geometric errors in each layer required pausing and restarting the print rather than real-time correction.
Legacy machines without standalone APIs required non-invasive camera-based data extraction, introducing potential OCR/image processing errors.
limitations: System currently limited to specific machines (Ultimaker S5, Stratasys F170, Mockpanel); expanding to CNC machines and robotic arms is future work.
Composite queries involving multiple agents are not fully handled; early work on decomposing complex queries shows promise but is not yet implemented.
Fine-tuning is computationally expensive and impractical for frequent updates as real-time data evolves.
The avatar operates in a static mode without VR immersion; VR integration is proposed for future work.
Audio processing costs ($15/1M characters) may limit scalability for high-volume voice interactions.

identified_research_gaps: Expanding the system to interface with a broader range of factory machine tools such as CNC machines and robotic arms
Integration of machine learning models with LLMs for more advanced diagnostics and predictive maintenance
Handling composite queries involving multiple agents through a supervisor agent capable of decomposing complex queries
Integration of virtual reality (VR) for immersive real-time visualization of the digital twin and interactive adjustment of machine parameters
Refining interoperability, scalability, and robustness of integrated LLM-IIoT-digital twin systems for enhanced decision-making, automation, and human-machine collaboration
